LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS BURGER BAR SAN FRANCISCO LLC., A LIMITED LIABILITY, HUBERT KELLER AND JAMES BEAUPARLANT'S MOTION TO DENY CLASS CERTIFICATION IS GRANTED ONLY AS TO PLEADED CLASS OTHERWISE DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-FILING IF PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT FILED A MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION BY JULY 20, 2013. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS HAVE RAISED SIGNIFICANT ISSUES CONCERNING THE CLASS DEFINITION AS PLEADED, ONE OF THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS, AND ONE OF CLASS COUNSEL, THEY HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT NO CLASS CAN BE CERTIFIED. THE COURT HAS VARIOUS TOOLS TO DEAL WITH THOSE ISSUES, IF NECESSARY. WHEN AND IF PLAINTIFFS FILE A MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION, THEY SHOULD ADDRESS THE CONCERNS RAISED IN THIS MOTION. THE OBJECTION TO THE VERESCHAGIN DECLARATION FILED JANUARY 4, 2013 IS SUSTAINED. THE DECLARATION WAS UNTIMELY FILED WITHOUT PERMISSION. THE COURT ALSO DID NOT CONSIDER THE UNAUTHORIZED AND UNTIMELY SUR-REPLY DECLARATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - GENERAL, FORM INTERROGAORIES - EMPLOYMENT LAW, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS TO ALL REQUESTS EXCEPT REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION 10 AND 20 AND FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 AND LIMITED TO ALL EMPLOYEES WHO ARE WAGE EARNERS ON AN HOURLY BASIS WITHOUT MANAGEMENT TITLE. WITHIN 14 DAYS NOTICE WILL BE SENT OUT BY THE DEFENDANTS FOR AN OPT OUT OPPORTUNITY. EMPLOYEES WILL BE GIVEN 25 DAYS TO OPT OUT AND IF THEY DO NOT DO SO, DISCOVERY WILL THEN BE PROVIDED. PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO IDENTITY AND CONTACT INFORMATION AND THE OTHER INFORMATION SOUGHT AS RELEVANT TO THE QUESTIONS OF CLASS COMPOSITION AND IDENTIFICATION OF WITNESSES. NAMED PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT ENTITLED TO PAYCHECK DEDUCTIONS AND PAYROLL TAX RECORDS FOR PUTATIVE CLASS MEMBERS AT THIS TIME, BUT THEY ARE ENTITLED TO THEIR OWN RECORDS CALLED FOR BY RFPS 10 AND 20, SO THOSE REQUESTS MUST BE RESPONDED TO AS TO THE NAMED PLAINTIFFS ONLY. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT ADEQUATELY RAISE FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 DURING THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS, SO THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THAT REQUEST. DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES WITHIN 20 DAYS. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $1125 ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF AND AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL TO BE PAID NO LATER THAN MARCH 1, 2013. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LINDA FORSTER, LICENSE NUMBER 13286 =302/PJB

Off Calendar Aug-16-2013 Vacated

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatoriesn - General, Form Interrogaories - Employment Law, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. NIMA NAMI OF THE NAMI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (949)610-8535 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. BRYAN VERESCHAGIN, TEL. NO. 834-5434 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KEVIN ALLEN OF THE MINAMI TAMAKI LLP, TEL. NO. 788-9000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
LINDA FORSTER, TEL. NO. (925) 548-1420, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - General, Form Interrogaories - Employment Law, Request For Production Of Documents, And Request For Sanctions is Granted as to all requests except Requests for Production 10 and 20 and Form Interrogatory 15.1 AND LIMITED TO ALL EMPLOYEES WHO ARE WAGE EARNERS ON AN HOURLY BASIS WITHOUT MANAGEMENT TITLE. Within 14 days notice will be sent out by the defendants for an opt out opportunity. Employees will be given 25 days to opt out and if they do not do so, discovery will then be provided. Plaintiffs are entitled to identity and contact information and the other information sought as relevant to the questions of class composition and identification of witnesses. Named Plaintiffs are not entitled to paycheck deductions and payroll tax records for putative class members at this time, but they are entitled to their own records called for by RFPs 10 and 20, so those requests must be responded to as to the named plaintiffs only. Plaintiffs did not adequately raise Form Interrogatory 15.1 during the meet and confer process, so the motion is denied as to that request. Defendant shall serve supplemental responses within 20 days. Monetary sanctions of $1125 are awarded to Plaintiff and against Defendants and their counsel to be paid no later than March 1, 2013. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Linda Forster, License Number 13286 =302/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Moton To Deny Class Certification
Off Calendar Feb-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRYAN VERESCHAGIN, TEL. NO. 834-5434 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. NIMA NAMI OF THE NAMI LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (949)610-8535 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KEVIN ALLEN OF THE MINAMI TAMAKI LLP, TEL. NO. 788-9000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS BURGER BAR SAN FRANCISCO LLC., A LIMITED LIABILITY, HUBERT KELLER AND JAMES BEAUPARLANT'S Motion To Deny Class Certification is Granted only as to pleaded class otherwise Denied without prejudice to re-filing if Plaintiffs have not filed a motion for class certification by July 20, 2013. Although Defendants have raised significant issues concerning the class definition as pleaded, one of the named plaintiffs, and one of class counsel, they have not shown that no class can be certified. The Court has various tools to deal with those issues, if necessary. When and if Plaintiffs file a motion for class certification, they should address the concerns raised in this motion. The objection to the Vereschagin Declaration filed January 4, 2013 is sustained. The declaration was untimely filed without permission. The Court also did not consider the unauthorized and untimely Sur-Reply Declaration. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)
